meta_title: For Students: project management to Solve can't debug code (Intermediate Checklist)
meta_description: Learn how to project management and overcome can't debug code. A Intermediate-level Checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: Project Management to Solve Debugging Woes
content.seo_description: Master debugging by integrating project management. Learn how to use Antigravity and Claude Code to streamline your workflow and build professional apps.
content.answer_block: Debugging isn't a lack of talent; it's a lack of process. By implementing structured project management, you break complex logic into trackable units. Use Antigravity for architectural oversight and Claude Code for iterative execution to transform chaotic errors into systematic, resolved tasks that build your portfolio and confidence.
content.intro:
You are staring at a screen that has become a graveyard of console errors, your heart sinking as the deadline approaches. You have the vision for your app, but the execution feels like trying to solve a Rubik’s cube in the dark. The frustration of being unable to debug isn't a sign that you aren't 'cut out for this'—it is simply a sign that you are building without a map.

True engineering is less about writing perfect code on the first try and more about managing complexity. When you adopt a rigorous project management framework, you stop fighting the code and start orchestrating it. Let’s pivot your workflow from frantic guessing to surgical precision using the industry’s most powerful AI-assisted tools.

steps.title: Architectural Mapping
steps.description: Before touching the code, map out your state transitions. This prevents logic errors before they manifest.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze my current project architecture and identify potential failure points in the state management flow. Suggest a modular structure.

steps.title: Task Decomposition
steps.description: Break the feature into micro-tasks. Large features are impossible to debug; small functions are trivial.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Break down the 'User Authentication' feature into 5 distinct, testable engineering tasks with clear acceptance criteria.

steps.title: Isolating the Error
steps.description: Use AI to trace the error back to the exact line of code rather than guessing the source.
steps.antigravity_prompt: I am receiving a 500 error in the API. Analyze the stack trace and pinpoint the exact file and function causing the conflict.
steps.claude_code_command: claude debug --file src/api/auth.ts --error '500 Internal Server Error'

steps.title: Iterative Implementation
steps.description: Apply fixes incrementally and verify each step to ensure no regression.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a unit test for the current function to ensure the bug remains fixed and doesn't resurface during future updates.

steps.title: Technical Documentation
steps.description: Document your fix to turn your debugging session into a learning milestone for your portfolio.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Summarize the bug, the root cause, and the resolution in a professional technical blog post format for my portfolio.

pro_tips: Treat every bug as a feature request for better error handling; don't just patch, build a safety net.
Use 'Rubber Ducking' with Claude Code: explain the logic out loud, and let the AI catch the flaw in your reasoning.
Prioritize 'Fail Fast' architectures where your code throws explicit errors early rather than failing silently.
Automate your testing pipeline; if it isn't tested, it's broken by definition.
Keep a 'Bug Journal' to track recurring patterns—this is how you level up from student to senior developer.
content.next_steps: Once you have successfully debugged your first module, deploy it to a staging environment and document the process in your GitHub README to showcase your problem-solving skills to recruiters.
content.conversion_block: You have the tools, the process, and the expertise now. The gap between where you are and where you want to be is just one project away. Stop spinning your wheels and start building with intent. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
